question_text: Which of the following is the main reason why fossil fuels are considered non-renewable energy resources?

options.label: A
options.text: They produce too much pollution when burned

options.label: B
options.text: They take millions of years to form, so they are used up faster than they are replaced

options.label: C
options.text: They are only found in certain countries

explanation: Fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas) are non-renewable because they take millions of years to form from dead organisms, while we consume them much faster than nature can replenish them. This makes them a finite resource on human timescales, unlike renewable resources such as solar or wind energy that can be continuously replenished.
